- Trump Media, which trades under the DJT ticker, closed at $45.16 per share, a loss of $4.77 per share.
- But the stock is still trading well below the more than $70 per share it opened at in its public trading debut on March 26, after Trump Media merged with Digital World Acquisition Corp.
Ihor Dusaniwsky, managing director of predictive analytics at S3 Partners, told CNBC that short interest in DJT shares is more than $147 million, with 3.55 million shares shorted.
"But over the last thirty days we've seen 2.8 million shares of short covering, worth $97 million, a -40% decline in total shares shorted."

- This bitcoin miner and Nvidia AI cloud partner's stock could go up 50%, Berenberg says"},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2024/04/03/trump-media-is-the-most-expensive-us-stock-to-short-by-far.html","title":"Trump Media is the most expensive U.S. stock to short — by far","content":"Investors who wanted to borrow Trump Media shares to sell them short on Wednesday would have had to pay financing costs of between 750% and 900% of the price of the stock annually, said Ihor Dusaniwsky, managing director of predictive analytics at S3 Partners.
That means a short seller of the DJT ticker who took a position Wednesday would have had to pay costs of between about $1 and $1.22 per day to the lenders.
To break even on a new trade after one month, a short seller would have to see the share price of Trump Media drop by more than $30.
In March, short sellers' positions in DWAC and then in Trump Media were down about $126 million in so-called mark-to-market losses, a drop of nearly 70% for the month.

DJT stock - Trump Media Pop Is Giving DJT Stock Short Sellers a Run for Their Money. According to S3 Partners, DJT is the most shorted SPAC in the U.S. based on its cost to borrow (CTB) fee of 157.57% as of yesterday, March 26. A total of 3.37 million shares worth $168.6 million were being shorted, equivalent to a short interest as a percentage of float of 11.69%. ## DJT Stock: Short Sellers Drive Up Cost to Borrow Fee. So, why is DJT’s CTB fee so high? However, due to the high CTB fee, the price of DJT stock will be more influenced by buyers and sellers of stock rather than short sellers. Trump Media is making a point of telling its shareholders how to prevent their stock from being loaned to short sellers — who bet the price of the shares will drop. The short-selling-prevention tips posted Wednesday on Trump Media’s website come as its DJT stock has sharply fallen in price since it began being public trading on March 26 — and as short sellers have taken a keen interest in the owner of the Truth Social app despite relatively high fees to finance such trades.
